Intracellular Niche in Erythrocytes and Endothelium (Cell-Penetrant Drug Requirement)

nodes.description: Bartonella bacilliformis depends on 70S-ribosome translation. Chloramphenicol binds the 50S subunit and blocks peptidyl transferase peptide-bond formation, arresting bacterial protein synthesis. This ribosomal target — shared with the alternative agent doxycycline (30S) — is why protein-synthesis inhibitors, not beta-lactams, anchor therapy.

evidence.snippet: The ribosome is one of the main antibiotic targets in the bacterial cell.

nodes.description: B. bacilliformis is a facultative intracellular pathogen that invades and persists within erythrocytes and vascular endothelial cells. Effective therapy requires an antibiotic that reaches the intracellular organism; chloramphenicol and doxycycline penetrate host cells, whereas beta-lactams cannot — the lifestyle gating that constrains drug choice beyond the ribosomal target.
